pattern: A bijective base-26 numeral system where each digit cycles A–Z, requiring a subtract-1-before-mod trick to avoid a zero digit.
hints: Unlike decimal, there is no zero digit here — A represents 1 and Z represents 26, so a direct modulo gives wrong results for multiples of 26.
Subtract 1 from the number before taking the remainder: that shifts Z (26) to index 25 so the letters map correctly.
Repeatedly peel off the last character with (n-1) % 26, prepend the corresponding letter, then set n = (n-1) // 26 until n reaches zero.

edge_inputs.description: first column maps to single letter A
column that is a power-of-26 boundary ZZ
large column number in the AAA range
exact multiple of 26 must yield Z not a carry with empty last digit
near-max value to verify no integer overflow in JS

stress.description: random column numbers across the full valid range
